name: Eekso — The Pitfall Prison
dmNotes:
THE HORROR FLOOR. One idea, four ways: the necromancer HARVESTS, and the building is plumbing. Tilted floors, gutters, warm pipes, all running downhill to Level 2. Plant the tilt early and never explain it.

Two entrances: dropped through a rug (start at 'The Landing') or the stairwell (start at 'The Prison'). If the party is split, run the dropped PC alone for a few minutes.

TONE: cold and industrial, not gross-out. The necromancer is TIDY. Nothing wasted. That's what disturbs.

Enemies here: sprinkle in whatever you've placed on the map — this scene stays out of your way.

music.label: Somebody's people
music.file: music/Tony Anderson - No Place on Earth.mp3

scriptText:
# The Pitfall Prison

{{music:dread}} {{amb:water-dripping|in=3s}}

> [!dm] **Start at 'The Landing' only if someone fell through a rug** (music: 'landing' instead). Stairwell entry → skip to *The Prison*.

## The Landing

{{music:landing}}

Thirty feet of falling in the dark — and you stop before the floor does.

The first thing is the smell: old iron, and under it something sweeter and much worse. The second thing is the pain — white, total, everywhere at once — and when your eyes work again you understand why. An iron spike stands out of the front of your shoulder. It went in through the back. There is another through your leg, just above the knee, and your own blood is already moving away from you along the floor — *downhill*, in a thin, purposeful line, like it knows where it's going.

When you try to move, the spikes hold you. You are pinned to the bottom of this place like everything else it keeps.

> [!dm] **3d6 bludgeoning + 2d10 piercing** (DC 13 Dex halves the spike damage only).
>
> - **Impaled** — speed 0 until they pull free; tearing loose costs half their movement

## The Prison

{{music:dread}}

- The stair bottoms out into a corridor of cells — iron bars in old stone, most doors open, hinges rusted through. A prison, honestly, before it was anything else.

- **West cells:** old bones. People who sat down in irons and stopped. Lifetimes ago.

- **East cells:** bodies *fresh enough to still be people*. Three or four. Slumped, grey, mouths open. **No wounds** — they look drained, because they are.

- The floor of each east cell **slopes gently toward a gutter** cut along the back wall

> [!dm] **The floors are tilted** — every room on this level, same direction. Never explain it.

### The Pawn

In the last cell on the east side, one of them lifts his head.

> [!dm] Alive in the strictest sense, and not in there. Flat voice, no affect, eyes not quite tracking. Questions → he **says it again**, identical, from the top.

He says:

**"Don't trust what you see in there. None of it is real. None of it is real. You can walk right through. It's all just paint and old bones and he wants you frightened — that's all he has. Don't trust what you see. You can walk right through."**

> [!dm] **It's a lie** — planted to walk them into the Art Room with their guard down.
>
> - **Insight DC 13:** he's not lying — he isn't *present* enough to lie. He's reciting.
> - **Insight DC 16 / Arcana DC 15:** the words aren't his. Something speaks through the shape of him.
> - **Medicine DC 12:** he should be dead. He's been kept, deliberately, at the edge.
> - Freed: walks where pushed, repeats the line. Dies within the hour, peacefully, mid-sentence.
> - Asked his name: a long pause — the only crack in it — then the line starts over.

### Cell Traps

> [!dm] Two or three, your placement. Old, from an earlier owner; the necromancer just never cleared them.
>
> **NO LOOT on this floor (Wes's call)** — anything worth taking went downstairs long ago. The traps guard nothing; that's the joke.
>
> - **Dart trap** in a door frame: Perception DC 14 · +6 to hit · 2d4 piercing + 2d6 poison (DC 13 Con half)
> - **Spike trap** under loose flagstones: Investigation DC 15 · DC 14 Dex or 2d10 piercing
> - **Glyph of warding** under a bunk, faded but live: Investigation DC 16 · DC 15 Dex · 4d8 necrotic

## The Plumbing

{{music:walk}}

- Deeper in, the building's real design shows: every floor **pitched** a few degrees, always the same direction

- Gutters where floor meets wall; grates in the corners; **pipes** — clay and iron, strapped at knee height — all heading the same way. Downhill. Downstairs. Toward Level 2.

- They are not empty. You can hear them.

> [!dm]
> - **Investigation DC 10:** every slope, gutter and pipe runs to the same place
> - The pipes are **warm to the touch**
> - Nothing connects to the river — a **closed circuit**, built by hand

## The Stacks

{{music:stacks}} {{amb:ghostly-loop|vol=25%|in=6s|until=section}}

The southwest storeroom has been filled the way a storeroom gets filled.

Bodies. Floor to ceiling, wall to wall, stacked in rows with walkways left between them. Old and dry at the bottom; fresher as they rise. Near the top, under grey blankets pulled up neat, are people who look like they were alive last week. Each one has a tube in the arm. Each tube runs to a line, each line to the gutter, and the gutter runs downhill.

And some of them are still breathing.

> [!dm]
> - **Medicine DC 12:** deeply unconscious, unwakeable, not going to recover
> - **Arcana DC 15:** held on the exact line between alive and not, by something continuous. Kill the caster and they go.
> - **If a player tries to save them:** they can end it here, one at a time, by hand. Don't punish it.

## The Grinding Room

{{amb:nox-old-mill|vol=35%|in=4s|until=section}}

- Off the stacks: a smaller room, and out of it a slow, patient, *turning* sound

- A hand-cranked mill — a village grain mill — and it has not been used for grain. Hopper full. Stone dark.

- Under the spout: sacks, filled and folded shut, stacked by size. What's in them is **bone, ground fine**.

- The sacks are labelled — not with names, with **dates**

- A ledger on a nail: tallies, weights, a running total. The columns balance. **Nothing has been wasted.** {{music:grief}}

- The crank is worn smooth and shiny where a hand goes

> [!dm] The sacks go downstairs — they're an *ingredient*. When they see what the Torm symbol is painted with, this room snaps into place.

## Onward — The Blocked Corridor

- East of the spike pit, the corridor to the far stair is **shut** — ceiling down, rubble wall to wall

- **Investigation DC 12:** the collapse is old and was never cleared. No way through in any reasonable time.

- The only other way on: a doorway south, and past it the darkness isn't flat. Something in it is *hanging*. It moves slightly. It makes a small dry sound.

> [!dm] The funnel — the rubble exists to make the Art Room the only door. They've just been told none of it is real. Switch to **'Eekso — The Art Room'**.

ideas.text: Every floor on this level is pitched a few degrees the same direction. Plant it in the cells, never explain it.
The pipes are warm to the touch
An impaled PC's own blood runs away from them downhill in a thin purposeful line, like it knows where it's going
Someone tucked the blankets in around the fresh bodies and straightened them
The bonemeal sacks are labelled with dates, not names, and the ledger's columns balance
Ask the pawn his name and there's a long pause — the only crack — then he restarts the line
Nothing here connects to the river. It's a closed circuit somebody built by hand.

entities.name: The pawn (last east cell)
entities.note: A recording, not a person. 'None of it is real — you can walk right through.' A lie to lower their guard for the Art Room. Dies within the hour if freed.

entities.name: The stacks
entities.note: Bodies floor to ceiling, tubed and drained. Some still breathing, kept deliberately.

entities.name: The grinding room
entities.note: Hand mill, bonemeal in dated sacks, a balanced ledger. The sacks are an ingredient for downstairs.

entities.name: The plumbing
entities.note: Pitched floors, gutters, warm pipes. All of it runs downhill to Level 2.

entities.name: Collapsed corridor
entities.note: Funnels the party into the Art Room — the only way on.
